findings.question: Q1-Q3
findings.detail: Scope, exclusions, and D1 ALWAYS/ASK FIRST/NEVER boundaries match D1 Constitution, D2 Specification, and D10 exactly.

findings.question: Q4
findings.detail: Six Ledger methods and four internal module boundaries match D10 Active Components and BUILDER_HANDOFF Section 3.

findings.question: Q5
findings.detail: Implementation in staging, gate artifacts in sawmill. CRITICAL_REVIEW_REQUIRED flag on 13Q path conflict resolved: prompt instruction is authoritative for gate artifacts.

findings.question: Q6
findings.detail: Event fields, hash format, canonical serialization rules, and four error codes match D4 contracts and examples.

findings.question: Q7
findings.detail: PC-001-ledger-core file inventory and SHA256 evidence plan match handoff Sections 5 and 9.

findings.question: Q8
findings.detail: Python stdlib + platform_sdk + immudb-through-abstraction matches handoff and D1 tooling constraints.

findings.question: Q9
findings.detail: 25+ tests, SC-001 through SC-011, four targeted commands plus full regression match handoff Sections 6 and 8.

findings.question: Q10
findings.detail: Names FMWK-002 as caller and FMWK-003/005/006 as consumers. Mechanical-only integration boundary is specific.

findings.question: Q11-Q12
findings.detail: Scope drift risk (semantic smuggling, D1 Art 2/6) and failure softening (no partial success, D1 Art 8) correctly identified.

findings.question: Q13
findings.detail: CRITICAL_REVIEW_REQUIRED flag on missing adversarial prompt list resolved: answers demonstrate genuine comprehension from D1-D8 rather than rote compliance.
